title: systemctl stop podman.service after each reset
body:
users were running into issues with system reset and invalid db configs
resolve this by restarting the podman user service each time we reset.

resolves #12076

Signed-off-by: cdoern <cdoern@redhat.com>

comments.body: All kinds of nasty red tests.  You might want to rebase as @mheon just did a reset PR and this might be helped by that, but I'm not sure.

seems that my version of systemctl does NOT have the same output as CI, womp womp.

Changing test condition to the output they have in common (meaning this will pass locally and on CI)
New changes are detected. LGTM label has been removed.

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
alright @Luap99 I am coming back to this one now. How do you think we should approach this?

I am not a fan of `podman --root ... --runroot ... system reset` potentially restarting the podman service which could use completely different directories.

I would still argue that it is the user responsibility to take care of this, the reported issue was with a manually started `podman system service` process so this patch will not even help there.

I am fine with dropping this if there is a workaround to fix it and if there is team disagreement over it. 

@rhatdan WDYT? do you think we should just leave this to the user?
